postData.contentHtml:
New Zealand cricket fans are celebrating a new record-holder as leg-spinner Ish Sodhi has officially become his country's all-time leading wicket-taker in T20 International cricket. The historic moment unfolded during the third T20I against Bangladesh in Dhaka, where Sodhi claimed his 165th wicket in the shortest format of the game, surpassing the formidable former pacer Tim Southee.

Sodhi's record-breaking delivery saw him dismiss Shamim Hossain, a pivotal moment in a rain-shortened match. Despite the game being reduced to 15 overs per side, Sodhi's crucial performance contributed significantly to New Zealand's six-wicket victory, which ultimately leveled the series 1-1. His spell of 1/22 from three overs underscored his impact on the game.

The match also showcased strong performances from other New Zealand bowlers, with Josh Clarkson bagging an impressive three wickets, and Nathan Smith and Ben Sears each contributing two wickets to the Blackcaps' commanding effort.

Simulated social sentiment surrounding Sodhi's achievement has been overwhelmingly positive, sparking engaging discussions among cricket enthusiasts. This milestone not only highlights Sodhi's consistent brilliance and dedication to the sport but also solidifies his reputation as one of New Zealand's finest T20 bowlers.
postData.title: Ish Sodhi Makes History: New Zealand's New King of T20I Wickets
postData.summary: New Zealand's leg-spinner Ish Sodhi has etched his name in the record books, becoming the country's all-time highest wicket-taker in T20 International cricket. This momentous achievement has been met with widespread positive sentiment among cricket fans.

sources.title: Which bowler has taken most wickets in T20Is? Ish Sodhi becomes New Zealand's all-time highest wicket taker, closes in on Rashid Khan
